content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

December 6, 1991

Honorable Charles Egan Goff
2 Marcela Ave.
San Francisco, CA 94116

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-91-501

Dear Judge Goff:

You have requested advice concerning your reporting
obligations under the conflict of interest disclosure provisions
of the Political Reform Act.1/

QUESTION

As a retired judge who has been serving on temporary
assignments, are you required to file Statements of Economic
Interests (Form 721)? If so, when are the statements due?

CONCLUSION

Once you have served 30 days or more in any 12-month period,
you must begin filing statements of economic interests with the
clerk of the court to which you have been assigned. You then must
file annual statements until your assignment ends. Once your
assignment ends and you do not receive a new assignment, you must
file a leaving office statement within 30 days of leaving your
last assignment.

_________________________
1/Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Judge Charles Goff
Page 2

FACTS

     You retired from San Francisco Municipal Court on June 4,
1990, and filed a leaving office statement.  Since July 9, 1991,
you have received assignments to various courts.

ANALYSIS

     The Act requires that judges file statements of economic
interests (Form 721).  (Section 87200-87204.)  However, Regulation
187242/ provides that retired judges are not required to file
statements of economic interests unless they serve or expect to
serve 30 days or more in any 12-month period.

     If you have served 30 days or more in any 12-month period
since the date of your retirement, you must file an assuming
office statement of economic interests (Form 721).  The "Date
Assumed Office" as indicated on the cover page of the Form 721
should be the date on which you served a total of 30 days since
your retirement.  The assuming office Form 721 must be filed no
later than 30 days after the date you determine to be your "date
assumed office."  If the filing deadline has passed, please file
your statement as soon as possible, and attach a letter explaining
why the statement is late.  The assuming office Form 721 should be
filed with the clerk of the court to which you were assigned on
the date you determine to be your "date assumed office."  The
clerk of the court will make and retain a copy and forward the
original statement to the Fair Political Practices Commission.

     After filing the assuming office statement, you must file
"annual" statements no later than March 1 of each year until you
are no longer accepting assignments.  At the time of your last
assignment, and when you do not anticipate accepting assignments
again, you must file a "leaving office" statement.  The leaving
office statement must be filed within 30 days of the termination
of your assignments.  Each of your required statements should be
filed with the clerk of the court to which you are assigned on the
date the statement is due.

_________________________

2/ Copies of Commission regulations and opinions are available in
many law libraries.  Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.

Judge Charles Goff
page 3


     If you have any questions or need further assistance, please
contact me at (916) 322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel

                                        [signature]
                                        by: Colleen McGee
                                            Political Reform Consultant
                                            Technical Assistance and
                                            Analysis Division
